title: OpRecord
description:
`ops/<op_id>.json` — the durable request identity, persisted (`0600`) BEFORE the first network send so
an uncertain write replays the SAME `op_id` (the server returns the byte-identical receipt — no
double-advance, no duplicate commit). It carries the full bound identity the device-op signature binds,
so a replay re-signs the identical frame. **Field-set pinned.**

bundle_digest.description: The candidate's byte-exact bundle digest (the consent hash) — bound by the signature.

candidate_commit.description: The built commit (`version_id`) this op publishes / reverts / reviews — bound by the signature.

display_name.description:
The skill's advisory display name (the author's folder name) sent alongside a publish/propose so a
replay re-sends the identical value. UNSIGNED — it names the follower's folder + the dashboard entry,
never the digest or the signed frame. `None` for a revert/review and for pre-existing WALs.

expected_generation.description: The `(epoch,seq)` this op's compare-and-set targets — bound by the signature.

good.description:
The GOOD version a `revert` restores (the wire `good`) — present only for a `Revert` op (the
server builds the forward commit from it; it is NOT the `candidate_commit`, so a replay must carry
it). `None` for every other op.

last_receipt.description: The stored terminal receipt, once one is known (the source of idempotent-retry truth).

op.description:
The operation kind (the device-op subtype is an integrity property — an approve never replays as a
reject).

skill_id.description: The skill this op targets — part of the device-op bound identity.

workspace_id.description: The workspace this op targets — part of the device-op bound identity.

Generation.description:
The internal anti-replay counter `(epoch, seq)`. NEVER rendered to a user; the
agent consumes `expected`/`current` on a CONFLICT.

OpKind.description:
The device-signed operation an [`OpRecord`] carries — a serde mirror of the kernel's `DeviceOp` (which
lives in `topos-core`, not a dependency of this crate). The client maps it 1:1 to `DeviceOp` when
re-signing a replayed op. snake_case on the wire/disk.

Receipt.description:
The ONE canonical receipt across all 11 outcomes — the durable idempotency record (present even
on failures): one stable receipt per op, identical on retry.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp (the plane stamps it; never an ambient clock in `topos-core`).

key_id.description: The signing key id that covered this op.

op_id.description: Client-minted UUIDv4, persisted before the first send.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

TerminalOutcome.description: The closed set of terminal outcomes the agent branches on. SCREAMING_SNAKE on the wire.

oneOf.description:
A direct `publish` under `review-required`: refused, uploads/opens **nothing**, carries the
`publish --propose` next-action.
Returned only for a successfully-opened proposal (an explicit `--propose`).
